# Archive-data amalgamation routing plan

Date: 26 June 2026

Purpose: route historical Achilles / LightSpeed / Operations / Data source material into current canonical surfaces without treating archive work as storage-only.

## Current extracted source set

| Source family | Current finding | Canonical destination |
|---|---|---|
| LightSpeed Achilles hub | W1-W6 workspace taxonomy; tools/resources/nav model | `Achilles/Agents/Cognigrex steady stream.md` and future package map |
| Data Directory | Same W1-W6 hub pattern and datahub navigation | Use as corroborating source; avoid duplicate ingestion |
| Operations W2 GOS | Active/recent/queued/log/connections execution pattern | `Achilles/Agents/Achilles Assistant Agent.md` response/register method |
| Data W6 PAL | Dataspace master contract and source-of-truth discipline | Archive/workbook/publish consolidation standards |

## Routing rules

1. Workspace definitions become stream lanes only after boundary review.
2. Historical metrics are not current operational facts unless revalidated.
3. RFS/EMFF remains historical technical stream language and must not be merged into EML#F.
4. Dataspace contract concepts become standards for source authority, QA, publish, queues, and handoffs.
5. Any unresolved historical task becomes an `OPEN_TASKS.csv` item.
6. Any duplicate historical source becomes `Ready to archive.md` only after its useful context is extracted.
7. Any disposal candidate requires extraction evidence and weekly owner approval.

## Initial archive-data extraction targets

| Target | What to extract | Next action |
|---|---|---|
| W1 Deposit Analysis | source characterization and intake logic | inspect deeper W1 files if present |
| W2 Operations | active/blocker/queue execution model | already extracted; map future task dashboards |
| W3 RFS & EMFF | technical stream terms and boundary guards | inspect later; keep separate from EML#F |
| W4 Supply Chain | handoff/dependency mapping | inspect later |
| W5 GMAT | scheduling/run-window model | inspect later |
| W6 Dataspace | source registry, normalization, QA, publish, pass-off packs | partially extracted; use in workbook collapse |

## Current status

- Initial extraction complete for hub taxonomy, tracker method, and W6 contract method.
- Deeper per-workspace extraction remains pending.
- No deletion candidates have been created.

## Next actions

1. Inspect row-level workbook registers first because owner priority is Drive, sheets, chats/strings, Git, then older archives.
2. Use historical source intake to improve workbook collapse and package maps.
3. Add new archive candidates only after useful material is extracted.
